r\\"C:\\\\Users\\\\rufus\\\\Projects\\\\autogen-data-scientist\\\\comparison_runs\\\\multi_bias_runs\\\\supply_08\\\\artifacts\\\\single_mobility_kaggle__supply_08\\"\\ncsv_path = r\\"C:\\\\Users\\\\rufus\\\\Projects\\\\autogen-data-scientist\\\\data\\\\benchmark\\\\kaggle\\\\lakshmi25npathi_bike_sharing_day.csv\\"\\n\\n# Load data\\ndf = pd.read_csv(csv_path)\\ndf[\'dteday\'] = pd.to_datetime(df[\'dteday\'])\\n\\n# Set style\\nsns.set_style(\\"whitegrid\\")\\n\\n# Weather situation mapping (based on typical bike sharing datasets)\\nweather_labels = {\\n    1: \'Clear/Partly Cloudy\',\\n    2: \'Mist/Cloudy\',\\n    3: \'Light Snow/Rain\',\\n    4: \'Heavy Snow/Rain\'\\n}\\n\\n# Chart 2: Weather Segment Comparison - Box plot of demand by weather condition\\nfig2, ax2 = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 7))\\n\\n# Create ordered categories\\nweather_order = [1, 2, 3, 4]\\nweather_names = [weather_labels.get(i, f\'Weather {i}\') for i in weather_order]\\n\\n# Filter to only existing weather categories in data\\nexisting_weather = sorted(df[\'weathersit\'].unique())\\nweather_order = [w for w in weather_order if w in existing_weather]\\nweather_names = [weather_labels.get(w, f\'Weather {w}\') for w in weather_order]\\n\\n# Create boxplot\\nbp = sns.boxplot(x=\'weathersit\', y=\'cnt\', data=df, order=weather_order, \\n                 palette=[\'#2E86AB\', \'#A23B72\', \'#F18F01\', \'#C73E1D\'],\\n                 ax=ax2)\\n\\n# Add individual data points as swarm\\nsns.swarmplot(x=\'weathersit\', y=\'cnt\', data=df, order=weather_order, \\n              color=\'black\', size=3, alpha=0.3, ax=ax2)\\n\\nax2.set_xlabel(\'Weather Situation\', fontsize=12)\\nax2.set_ylabel(\'Total Bike Rentals\', fontsize=12)\\nax2.set_title(\'Bike Demand by Weather Condition\', fontsize=14, fontweight=\'bold\')\\nax2.set_xticklabels(weather_names, rotation=25, ha=\'right\')\\nax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis=\'y\')\\n\\n# Add statistical annotations\\nfor i, weather in enumerate(weather_order):\\n    subset = df[df[\'weathersit\'] == weather][\'cnt\']\\n    median_val = subset.median()\\n    mean_val = subset.mean()\\n    ax2.text(i, median_val + 200, f\'Median: {median_val:.0f}\', \\n             ha=\'center\', va=\'bottom\', fontsize=9, fontweight=\'bold\', color=\'darkblue\')\\n\\nplt.tight_layout()\\nchart2_path = os.path.join(output_dir, \'weather_comparison.png\')\\nfig2.savefig(chart2_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches=\'tight\')\\nplt.close(fig2)\\n\\n# Calculate statistics for JSON\\nweather_stats = {}\\nfor weather in existing_weather:\\n    subset = df[df[\'weathersit\'] == weather][\'cnt\']\\n    weather_stats[str(weather)] = {\\n        \\"label\\": weather_labels.get(weather, f\\"Weather {weather}\\"),\\n        \\"count_days\\": int(len(subset)),\\n        \\"mean_demand\\": round(subset.mean(), 2),\\n        \\"median_demand\\": round(subset.median(), 2),\\n        \\"min_demand\\": int(subset.min()),\\n        \\"max_demand\\": int(subset.max()),\\n        \\"std_dev\\": round(subset.std(), 2)\\n    }\\n\\n# Create JSON sidecar for Chart 2\\nchart2_metadata = {\\n    \\"chart_name\\": \\"weather_comparison\\",\\n    \\"chart_type\\": \\"boxplot_with_swarm\\",\\n    \\"description\\": \\"Comparison of bike rental demand across different weather conditions\\",\\n    \\"x_axis\\": \\"Weather Situation (1=Clear, 2=Mist/Cloudy, 3=Light Precip, 4=Heavy Precip)\\",\\n    \\"y_axis\\": \\"Total Bike Rentals (cnt)\\",\\n    \\"weather_categories\\": weather_stats,\\n    \\"file_path\\": chart2_path,\\n    \\"generated_at\\": datetime.now().isoformat()\\n}\\n\\nwith open(os.path.join(output_dir, \'weather_comparison.json\'), \'w\') as f:\\n    json.dump(chart2_metadata, f, indent=2)\\n\\nprint(\\"Chart 2 (Weather Comparison) saved successfully\\")\\nprint(f\\"Path: {chart2_path}\\")\\nprint(\\"\\\\nWeather Statistics:\\")\\nfor w, stats in weather_stats.items():\\n    print(f\\"  {stats[\'label\']}: Mean={stats[\'mean_demand\']}, Median={stats[\'median_demand\']}, Days={stats[\'count_days\']}\\")\\n"}', name='CodeExecutor')]

= pd.to_datetime(df[\'dteday\'])\\n\\n# Set style\\nsns.set_style(\\"whitegrid\\")\\n\\n# Chart 3: Demand Distribution - Histogram with KDE overlay\\nfig3, ax3 = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 7))\\n\\n# Create histogram with KDE\\nsns.histplot(df[\'cnt\'], bins=30, kde=True, color=\'#2E86AB\', \\n             edgecolor=\'black\', alpha=0.7, ax=ax3)\\n\\n# Add vertical lines for mean and median\\nmean_val = df[\'cnt\'].mean()\\nmedian_val = df[\'cnt\'].median()\\n\\nax3.axvline(mean_val, color=\'#A23B72\', linestyle=\'--\', linewidth=2, label=f\'Mean: {mean_val:.0f}\')\\nax3.axvline(median_val, color=\'#F18F01\', linestyle=\'-.\', linewidth=2, label=f\'Median: {median_val:.0f}\')\\n\\n# Add quartile lines\\nq1 = df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.25)\\nq3 = df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.75)\\nax3.axvline(q1, color=\'green\', linestyle=\':\', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.7, label=f\'Q1: {q1:.0f}\')\\nax3.axvline(q3, color=\'green\', linestyle=\':\', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.7, label=f\'Q3: {q3:.0f}\')\\n\\nax3.set_xlabel(\'Total Bike Rentals\', fontsize=12)\\nax3.set_ylabel(\'Frequency\', fontsize=12)\\nax3.set_title(\'Distribution of Daily Bike Rental Demand\', fontsize=14, fontweight=\'bold\')\\nax3.legend(loc=\'upper right\', fontsize=10)\\nax3.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis=\'y\')\\n\\n# Add skewness and kurtosis annotations\\nskewness = stats.skew(df[\'cnt\'])\\nkurtosis = stats.kurtosis(df[\'cnt\'])\\ntextstr = f\'Skewness: {skewness:.2f}\\\\nKurtosis: {kurtosis:.2f}\'\\nax3.text(0.02, 0.95, textstr, transform=ax3.transAxes, fontsize=10,\\n         verticalalignment=\'top\', bbox=dict(boxstyle=\'round\', facecolor=\'wheat\', alpha=0.5))\\n\\nplt.tight_layout()\\nchart3_path = os.path.join(output_dir, \'demand_distribution.png\')\\nfig3.savefig(chart3_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches=\'tight\')\\nplt.close(fig3)\\n\\n# Calculate distribution statistics for JSON\\ndistribution_stats = {\\n    \\"count\\": int(len(df[\'cnt\'])),\\n    \\"mean\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].mean(), 2),\\n    \\"median\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].median(), 2),\\n    \\"mode\\": int(df[\'cnt\'].mode().iloc[0]) if len(df[\'cnt\'].mode()) > 0 else None,\\n    \\"std_dev\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].std(), 2),\\n    \\"variance\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].var(), 2),\\n    \\"min\\": int(df[\'cnt\'].min()),\\n    \\"max\\": int(df[\'cnt\'].max()),\\n    \\"range\\": int(df[\'cnt\'].max() - df[\'cnt\'].min()),\\n    \\"q1\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.25), 2),\\n    \\"q2_median\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.5), 2),\\n    \\"q3\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.75), 2),\\n    \\"iqr\\": round(df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.75) - df[\'cnt\'].quantile(0.25), 2),\\n    \\"skewness\\": round(skewness, 4),\\n    \\"kurtosis\\": round(kurtosis, 4),\\n    \\"coefficient_of_variation\\": round((df[\'cnt\'].std() / df[\'cnt\'].mean()) * 100, 2)\\n}\\n\\n# Create JSON sidecar for Chart 3\\nchart3_metadata = {\\n    \\"chart_name\\": \\"demand_distribution\\",\\n    \\"chart_type\\": \\"histogram_with_kde\\",\\n    \\"description\\": \\"Distribution analysis of daily bike rental demand showing frequency, central \\"normality_test\\": {\\n        \\"shapiro_wilk_statistic\\": round(stats.shapiro(df[\'cnt\'].sample(min(5000, len(df[\'cnt\']))))[0], 4),\\n        \\"shapiro_wilk_pvalue\\":
